response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source GIS platform, Green Map work, FOSS4G speaker) and real systems-level engineering with low-latency, high-throughput services in DLang at Sociomantic, which conceptually transfers to routing pipeline work. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings, and his only professional C++ experience is a seven-month stint ending in 2015; the last several years have centered on TypeScript/Node web development. He also has no routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance experience specifically, making him a stretch for this particular pipeline role despite adjacent strengths.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Low-latency distributed systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching, millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, Linux and Docker fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (brief 2014-2015 role); no Rust
No navigation, routing, or map-tiling pipeline experience
Recent decade of work is predominantly TypeScript/Node web and product development, not systems programming
